purpose: Help the PPT AI Agent choose a copyable TSX blueprint first, derive it into slides/*.tsx, and then edit the final slide implementation directly.

source_theme.temp_brief: Dark analytics comparison deck for China, Japan, and Korea with dashboards, timelines, and executive summaries.
visual_language: dark slate cover and closing pages
blue primary accent with teal and indigo secondary accents

rules: Start here by selecting the blueprint page closest to the content structure.
Copy or derive the selected blueprint into ./slides before adapting it for a concrete deck.
Do not paste source HTML into TSX; rebuild pages from reusable components and editable text.
group.json.layouts and manifest.json source.path must only reference this directory.
AI may edit TSX here directly to adjust layout, component composition, and content mapping.

components.role: Reusable visual components shared across pages.
rules: Put cross-page canvas, card, chart shell, comparison, and timeline components here.
Do not hide page-specific content decisions inside generic components.

data.role: Demo or concrete deck content data.
rules: JSON carries content only, not page-structure decisions.
If layout or component choice is wrong, edit slides/*.tsx.

theme.role: Shared color, typography, and visual token definitions.

blueprints.name: Cover Analytics
blueprints.composition_guidance: Use for the opening page. Preserve the dark analytics posture, background image wash, large subject-versus-subject headline, concise report scope, publisher, and date.

blueprints.name: Executive Summary Dashboard
suitable_for: KPI overview with one primary chart
avoid_for: more than three headline KPI cards
blueprints.composition_guidance: Use when the page needs three headline metrics, one grouped bar chart with short interpretation notes, and a compact dark outlook panel. Keep business claims in JSON data and edit TSX only for structure or component changes.

blueprints.name: Growth Trend Comparison
suitable_for: one primary line chart with left-side interpretation
blueprints.composition_guidance: Use when the page centers on one long-horizon multi-series line chart and needs concise entity cards to explain peaks, recent averages, volatility, and convergence. Keep factual claims and source notes in JSON data.

blueprints.name: Population Structure Comparison
suitable_for: market share or portfolio composition benchmark
paired donut charts with one center metric per entity
avoid_for: pages requiring more than five composition segments
blueprints.composition_guidance: Use when the page compares two entities by percentage composition. Keep the shared segment legend editable outside the chart screenshot, keep factual values in JSON data, and use TSX edits only for structural layout changes.

blueprints.name: Technology Capability Dashboard
suitable_for: dashboard pages with four paired metrics
sector advantage analysis with one strategic insight panel
avoid_for: pages requiring long prose or table-heavy evidence
blueprints.composition_guidance: Use when the page needs a high-density comparison dashboard: four paired circular metric cards across the top, sector advantage bars on the lower left, and a compact strategic divergence panel on the lower right. Keep business values in JSON data; edit TSX for structure only.

blueprints.name: Business Practice Matrix
suitable_for: business culture or operating practice matrix
partner, market, or country engagement playbook
three to six comparison dimensions with concise row-level contrast points
avoid_for: large tables requiring long paragraph cells
blueprints.composition_guidance: Use when the page needs a structured qualitative matrix: one dark analytics header, two entity lanes, a left dimension column, three to six row-level contrast points, and a short implication bar. Keep concrete entity names and business claims in JSON data.

blueprints.name: Closing Analytics
blueprints.composition_guidance: Use as the final page. Preserve the dark analytics background, concise closing headline, two short next-step callouts, and contact/date metadata. Do not use it for analytical body slides.

source_signals: top KPI cards plus chart panels

selection_guide: Choose executive-summary-dashboard for post-cover summary pages that need three KPI cards plus a primary grouped bar chart.
Choose technology-capability-dashboard for two-entity capability pages that need four circular comparison metrics, sector advantage bars, and a strategic divergence panel.
Choose business-practice-matrix for two-entity qualitative comparisons across several dimensions, especially business culture, operating model, partner practices, or market-entry engagement style.
Choose growth-trend-comparison for long-run multi-series line charts with left-side entity statistic cards and a short strategic interpretation.
Choose population-structure-comparison for two-entity composition pages such as age distribution, population structure, market share, portfolio mix, or segment allocation.
Choose cover-analytics for the first page or an intentional title reset.
Choose closing-analytics for the final page, thank-you page, decision ask, or next-step wrap-up.
Use executive-summary-dashboard's Future Outlook panel when a compact timeline is enough; add a dedicated timeline-analysis blueprint before registering timeline-only pages.
For other body pages, future work should add metric-dashboard, comparison-matrix, timeline-analysis, and agenda-summary blueprints before registering them.
Keep source HTML as reference only; pages should be rebuilt from TSX components with editable text and data-driven content.
agent_workflow_notes: Read catalog.json first and choose the blueprints/*.tsx file closest to the content structure.
Copy or derive the selected blueprint TSX into slides/*.tsx, then edit the final page directly.
manifest.json source.path should only point to slides/*.tsx.
data/*.json should carry content only; if structure, spacing, or components are unsuitable, edit slides/*.tsx.
Do not paste .tmp/Template HTML verbatim into TSX.
